message:
test(lifecycle): semantic fail-open contract assertion (ibuuh.11)

ibuuh.11 contract row: 'Bootstrap semantic assets and verify live
default-hybrid behavior'. The core fail-open invariant the bead wants
validated — when the semantic model is NOT installed, cass must report
state.semantic.available=false with fallback_mode='lexical' AND surface
a non-empty operator hint explaining the situation. Agents read this
block to decide whether to wait for semantic or proceed with lexical;
silent drift breaks every hybrid-preferred flow.

New test semantic_readiness_reports_lexical_fallback_when_models_absent
parses cass health --json against an isolated empty HOME and asserts:
 * state.semantic.available == false
 * state.semantic.can_search == false
 * state.semantic.fallback_mode == 'lexical' (exact string match; not
   empty or a placeholder — the fail-open contract requires an explicit
   tier identifier agents can route on)
 * state.semantic.hint is non-empty (operator-facing guidance)

This is the assertion-level counterpart to the existing ilnj9 health
golden that freezes the whole block's byte shape; this one catches
semantic-specific regressions of the fail-open contract fields even if
the wider golden gets intentionally updated.

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix: 62/62 pass.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.11; bead stays
open for the bootstrap/live-hybrid half that requires real semantic
assets (downstream of mot85/fsqlite + model install).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
